nameMeanings.name: McLean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Eáin, ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) John’. Compare Irish McAloon and McGlone .

History: This is the name of a major Scottish clan traditionally associated with the island of Mull, which claims descent from Gille Eáin na Tuaidh (‘Gillean of the Battleaxe’), who lived in the 13th century.

variants.name: Lean
meanings: English (mainly Devon): nickname for a thin or lean person, from Middle English lene ‘lean’ (Old English hlǣne).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 林, see   Lin   1.

variants.name: McClain
meanings: Scottish and Manx: variant of   McLean  .

variants.name: McClaine
meanings: Scottish and Manx: variant of   McLean  .

variants.name: McAloon
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Ghiolla Eóin ‘son of the servant of (Saint) John’. Compare McGlone and McLean .

nameMeanings.name: Alut
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An ancient Welsh name, indirectly related to Alan , of which it is now generally taken to be the Welsh equivalent. It is borne in the Mabinogi by Alun of Dyfed, a character mentioned in passing several times. It is also a river-name and a regional name in Wales, sometimes spelled Alyn. Alun was adopted as a bardic name by John Blackwell ( 1797–1840 ) and became popular as a result of his fame. It is now used increasingly outside Wales as an alternative spelling of Alan .
